rumusan: A very proud koala challenges a patient puppy to a race in the Green Forest. Because of his arrogance, he makes mistakes and loses to the steady competitor.
lesson: Arrogance leads to failure, while humility, patience, and steady focus lead to success.

title: The Mystery of the Missing Notebook
author: Barbara Martin
rumusan: Ben's favorite school notebook goes missing right before class. He and his friends search the school yard and find it with a funny, unexpected twist.
lesson: Working together as a team helps solve problems faster and makes the process enjoyable.

title: The Active and Healthy Little Rabbit
author: Richard Thompson
rumusan: This book features an active rabbit who does morning exercises daily in the Sunny Meadow. He encourages his lazy friends to join him to stay healthy.
lesson: Exercising regularly and eating healthy foods are key factors for a long and energetic life.

title: A New Friend from Happy Garden
author: Susan Garcia
rumusan: Tells the story of a new student who moves to the school from Happy Garden. Sam welcomes him warmly and helps him find his way around the classroom.
lesson: Being friendly and welcoming to newcomers creates a harmonious and inclusive environment.

title: Ruby's Creative Workshop
author: Joseph Martinez
rumusan: Ruby is a creative kid who loves to repair broken school bag instead of throwing them away. He repurposes old items to save money for his family.
lesson: Creativity and recycling help reduce waste and teach us the value of resourcefulness.

title: The Story of the Brave Little Squirrel
author: Emily Carter
rumusan: This book tells the heartwarming story of a little squirrel named Barnaby who was very brave. When he found a lost colored pencil in the Sunny Hill, he went on a long journey to return it to its rightful owner.
lesson: We should always practice the value of bravery and do the right thing, even when no one is watching.

title: Emma and the Mystery of the Magic Old Bicycle
author: Sarah Collins
rumusan: Follows the journey of a young boy named Emma who discovers a magical old bicycle near the Cozy Town. The magic object helps him with his daily chores but teaches him that honest effort is better than magic.
lesson: Relying on shortcut methods is never sustainable; hard work and self-reliance are the keys to true success.

title: The Great Adventure of Monkey in Crystal Lake
author: Anna Brooks
rumusan: Join a clever little monkey as it embarks on an exciting adventure across the beautiful Crystal Lake. Along the way, the animal cooperates with other creatures to protect their forest home from danger.
lesson: Unity and teamwork make it much easier to solve big problems and overcome unexpected challenges.

title: Lily's Dream of Having a Soccer Ball
author: Michael Reed
rumusan: Lily dreams of owning a new soccer ball to bring to school. With the guidance of his parents, he begins to save his allowance daily and helps with chores to achieve his goal.
lesson: Patience, financial discipline, and steady effort are essential when working toward a personal goal.

title: The Little Lion Looks for a True Friend
author: Helen Ward
rumusan: A lonely little lion travels all the way to Rainbow Creek in search of new friends. During the trip, the animal learns how polite words, sharing, and good manners make a big difference.
lesson: Good manners and kind words are the main foundation of any lasting and meaningful friendship.

title: Mystery of the Hidden Path in Green Forest
author: Peter Grant
rumusan: A group of school friends led by Anna discovers a hidden path behind the Green Forest. Together, they work to solve ancient riddles and uncover the old history of their neighborhood.
lesson: Positive curiosity drives us to learn history and discover new useful knowledge about our world.

title: A Very Busy Day for Ryan
author: Laura Hill
rumusan: This story follows the busy day of Ryan, who happily helps his mother clean the house, water the garden, and help an elderly neighbor in Peaceful Valley.
lesson: Being helpful to our family and local community brings happiness to ourselves and joy to others.

title: The Greedy Bee Learns to Share
author: Rachel Green
rumusan: A selfish little bee refuses to share food and toys with other animals in Sunny Meadow. When he gets stuck in a tricky situation, he realizes how important sharing is.
lesson: Being generous and willing to share blessings will bring happiness and strengthen friendships.

title: Toby's Bamboo Savings Jar
author: John Smith
rumusan: This educational story follows Toby as he learns about money management. He uses a handmade bamboo jar to save money to buy a wooden box he wants.
lesson: Saving money from a young age helps us develop independence and understand the true value of goods.

title: The Bravery of a Tiny Duck
author: Mary Johnson
rumusan: Tells the story of a very tiny duck who has a big heart. He saves his friends in Dream School from a large predator by using his clever wits instead of strength.
lesson: Clever thinking and timely courage are far more effective than mere physical strength.

title: A Special Gift for Max
author: David Brown
rumusan: Focuses on Max, who receives a special colored pencil from his grandfather. The gift is not just a toy, but contains a handwritten letter with valuable life advice.
lesson: We must cherish every gift we receive, especially the wisdom and advice passed down by elders.

title: The Bear Guarding Cozy Town
author: James Wilson
rumusan: A little bear is sad to see the beautiful Cozy Town filled with plastic waste. He organizes a big cleanup campaign with all the forest animals to restore nature.
lesson: Taking care of the environment and reducing waste is a shared duty to protect our health.

title: The School Talent Show at Crystal Lake
author: Robert Taylor
rumusan: Zac signs up for a creative talent competition at Crystal Lake. Although he struggles at first, he keeps practicing diligently until he wins a special prize.
lesson: Success is built on high determination, regular practice, and refusing to give up easily.

title: Being Honest Pays Off for Grace
author: Linda Thomas
rumusan: During school hours, Grace accidentally breaks a soccer ball belonging to his classmate. Despite being scared, he decides to tell the truth and apologize.
lesson: Honesty and admitting mistakes build deep trust and respect in any friendship.

title: Lucas's Vacation in the Countryside
author: Elizabeth White
rumusan: Follows Lucas as he spends his school holidays at his grandparents' cozy house in Rainbow Creek. He learns about herbal plants and local community values.
lesson: Valuing family traditions and spending time with grandparents strengthens our family bonds.

author: William Harris
